methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Growth → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.2x) to $174M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Defense & Security (found 0 same-stage, 1 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 24 same-sector peers exist in Defense & Security, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in Defense & Dual-use
Public-co frontier-tech comp in Defense & Dual-use

data.narrative:
Govini, a Growth-stage defense and security software company that has raised $174M to date, operates within a fragmented frontier-tech sector. The broader Defense & Security universe comprises 2 public comparables (DRSHF, IDEAFORGE.NS) and 8 private peers including Anduril Industries, Shield AI, and Flock Safety, though none are sufficiently proximate on stage and valuation to support reliable peer-median analysis.

Given the absence of a robust narrow peer set, valuation relies on stage-progression methodology. Applying a 1.2x step-up multiplier typical of Growth-stage advancement to the $174M total capital raised yields an implied valuation of $209M. This method treats cumulative fundraising as a proxy baseline and incorporates expected value accretion tied to stage progression.

The analysis supports a range of $177M to $240M, with a median estimate of $209M. The $177M floor reflects a conservative interpretation of the capital-raised baseline; the $240M ceiling incorporates upside case assumptions within the step-up framework. Key uncertainties include the absence of revenue and profitability data, limited direct peer comparables at equivalent stage and scale, and the cyclical nature of defense spending. Any valuation within this band remains contingent on forward verification of unit economics, customer concentration, and contract backlog visibility.
